summary: The test suite covers all four primary tag-generation rules (main/latest, pr-{number}, v{semver}, feature branch sanitization) and dedicates a full Describe block to sanitization edge cases (slashes, underscores, dots, leading/trailing/consecutive dashes, mixed case). Workflow-structure tests verify file existence, trigger types, shell type, script references, and actionlint. Gaps in rigor include: no test that a PR number still wins when semver GitTags are also provided; master branch is checked for 'latest' but 'master-{sha}' is never asserted; a CommitSha shorter than 7 chars is never exercised despite the Math.Min guard; and the entry-point / env-var parsing path is untested. Design is clean—BeforeAll dot-sources once, Describe blocks map 1-to-1 to requirements, $script: scoping in the Workflow Describe block is correct for Pester v5, and test names are descriptive. Workflow-structure tests rely on raw regex over YAML text, which is slightly fragile but pragmatic given no native YAML module. Overall the suite would reliably catch regressions on the happy path and most sanitization cases, but could miss interaction-rule bugs and boundary conditions.
